channel.title: AI Builder's Field Guide — Recently Added
channel.description: A daily-curated wiki of the tools, models, and patterns that AI power-users and builders actually reach for.

item.title: MemPalace
item.description: Open-source local AI memory system — stores conversation history as verbatim text with semantic retrieval. — Hits 96.6% R@5 on LongMemEval with zero API calls. If you want agent memory that doesn't leak to a vendor, this is the open-source baseline.

item.title: OB1
item.description: Self-hosted shared memory database — lets multiple AI tools read and write one persistent memory without vendor lock-in. — Bridges the memory silos: Claude, Cursor, and ChatGPT each have their own. OB1 is the memory backbone that unifies them.

item.title: You.com Search API
item.description: Web search API engineered for AI agents — 300ms p99 latency, real-time indexing across 10M+ news sources. — When you need fresh web grounding inside an agent and Tavily or Exa feels slow, You.com's latency is the differentiator.

item.title: DeepResearch-Bench
item.description: Open benchmark and leaderboard for deep-research agents (Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, and OSS) — hosted by muset-ai on Hugging Face Spaces. — If you're shipping a research agent or deciding which deep-research product to integrate, this is the public scoreboard to read first.

item.title: Zed AI
item.description: Rust-native code editor with agentic editing, real-time collaboration, and the open Agent Client Protocol (ACP). — The performance-first alternative to Cursor and Windsurf. 120 fps render, open prediction model (Zeta), and ACP make agent behavior auditable.

item.title: Conductor
item.description: Mac app that runs a fleet of Claude Code and Codex agents in parallel git worktrees, then helps you review and merge their work. — Single-machine multi-agent orchestration with native macOS UX. The 'one developer, many parallel agents' workflow distilled into a desktop app.

item.title: Paseo
item.description: Unified runner for Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and others — accessible from phone, desktop, or terminal. — If you switch between coding agents and devices, Paseo is the connector. Picks up where Claude.ai's mobile leaves off for power users.

item.title: Sandcastle
item.description: TypeScript library by Matt Pocock for orchestrating AI coding agents in isolated sandboxes — one function call handles lifecycle and branch merging. — If you're building a product that spawns coding agents, Sandcastle removes the boilerplate around containers and merge UX.
